class.name: EditorContextMenuPlugin
class.brief_description: Plugin for adding custom context menus in the editor.
class.description: [EditorContextMenuPlugin] allows for the addition of custom options in the editor's context menu. see [enum ContextMenuSlot] for the list of available areas where context menu plugins are supported.

method.name: _get_menu_options
method.description: Called when creating a context menu, custom options can be added by using the [method add_context_menu_item] or [method add_context_menu_item_from_shortcut] functions. [param data] contains the context data, which differs depending on the context menu slot. See the descriptions of [enum ContextMenuSlot] for more information.

method.name: _popup_menu
method.description: [b]Note:[/b] Implementing this method makes the shortcut callbacks receive legacy data (for compatibility reasons).

method.name: add_context_menu_item
method.description: Add custom option to the context menu of the plugin's specified slot. [param callback] should be a method that takes a single argument of type [Dictionary]. It will be called when the option is activated and the data received in the callback is the same as in [method _get_menu_options]. [codeblock] func _get_menu_options(data): add_context_menu_item("File Custom options", handle, ICON) [/codeblock] If you want to assign shortcut to the menu item, use [method add_context_menu_item_from_shortcut] instead.

method.name: add_context_menu_item_from_shortcut
method.description:
Add custom option to the context menu of the plugin's specified slot. The option will have the [param shortcut] assigned and reuse its callback. The shortcut has to be registered beforehand with [method add_menu_shortcut]. [codeblock] func _init(): add_menu_shortcut(SHORTCUT, handle)
func _get_menu_options(paths): add_context_menu_item_from_shortcut("File Custom options", SHORTCUT, ICON) [/codeblock]

method.name: add_context_submenu_item
method.description:
Add a submenu to the context menu of the plugin's specified slot. The submenu is not automatically handled, you need to connect to its signals yourself. Also the submenu is freed on every popup, so provide a new [PopupMenu] every time. [codeblock] func _get_menu_options(data): var popup_menu = PopupMenu.new() popup_menu.add_item("Blue") popup_menu.add_item("White") popup_menu.id_pressed.connect(_on_color_submenu_option)
add_context_submenu_item("Set Node Color", popup_menu) [/codeblock]

method.name: add_menu_shortcut
method.description: Registers a shortcut associated with the plugin's context menu. This method should be called once (e.g. in the plugin's [method Object._init]). [param callback] will be called when user presses the specified [param shortcut] while the menu's context is in effect (e.g. FileSystem dock is focused). Callback should take single data [Dictionary], same as in [method add_context_menu_item]. [codeblock] func _init(): add_menu_shortcut(SHORTCUT, handle) [/codeblock]

constants.constant: Context menu of Scene dock. The data [Dictionary] contains: [code]&"selected_nodes": Array[Node][/code] - The list of currently selected nodes (same as [method EditorSelection.get_top_selected_nodes]).
Context menu of FileSystem dock. The data [Dictionary] contains: [code]&"selected_files": PackedStringArray[/code] - The list of selected files.
Context menu of Script editor's script tabs. The data [Dictionary] contains: [code]&"selected_script": Script[/code] - The currently selected script. Can be [code]null[/code].

Context menu of Script editor's code editor. The data [Dictionary] contains: [code]&"code_edit": CodeEdit[/code] - The code editor instance. [code]&"file_path": String[/code] - Path to the currently edited file.
Context menu of scene tabs. The data [Dictionary] contains: [code]&"selected_scene": String[/code] - Scene path of the selected/clicked scene tab. Empty if empty space was clicked.

Context menu of the 2D editor's viewport. The data [Dictionary] contains: [code]&"hovered_nodes": Array[CanvasItem][/code] - The [CanvasItem] nodes under the cursor. [code]&"mouse_position": Vector2[/code] - The local mouse position in the 2D scene.

Context menu of an editor property in the inspector. The data [Dictionary] contains: [code]&"property": EditorProperty[/code] - The clicked [EditorProperty] object. [code]&"object": Object[/code] - The object edited by the inspector. [code]&"property_name": StringName[/code] - The name of the object's property that was clicked.
